description: Letter from a physician to a Kohen expressing thanks for condolences. At the end he writes a prescription for the growing of hair on skin where none would grow. The letter is in Judaeo-Arabic, but he introduces the prescription with a line of Arabic. (Information from Mediterranean Society, I, 253, 578.) "Let a black raven be taken. Let it be burned, pulverized, and kneaded with oil, and let it be applied to the spot on which no hair grows. It will grow with God's permission." Translation from Goitein's note card #27141.
